data.title: American Rebel approves 1-for-20 reverse stock split
data.description:
• American Rebel (NASDAQ:AREB) Board of Directors approved a 1-for-20 reverse stock split of the company’s common stock.
  • The company’s common stock will begin trading on a reverse-split adjusted basis at the opening of the market on Friday, October 3, 2025.
  • As of October 1, 2025, the company had 11,272,815 shares outstanding.

data.title: SU Group Holdings regains compliance with minimum publicly held shares and minimum bid requirements
data.description:
• SU Group Holdings (NASDAQ:SUGP) has announced Nasdaq has notified that the company has regained compliance with the minimum bid price requirement and the publicly held shares requirement in Nasdaq listing rule.
  • Accordingly, the hearing before the Hearings Panel scheduled to take place on October 21, 2025, has been cancelled due to mootness. 
  • SUGP +3.8% premarket to $5.93
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OppFi announces new $150 million revolving credit facility
data.description:
• OppFi (NYSE:OPFI) has closed a new $150 million revolving credit facility among one of its subsidiaries and funds managed by Castlelake L.P., replacing a prior facility.
  • The new facility has a four-year term and represents a significant improvement in financing costs, with a reduction in the interest rate from SOFR + 7.5% to SOFR + 6.0%.
  • The credit facility is designed to support the Company's ongoing growth in receivables and further its mission of providing credit access to millions of Americans who mainstream financial institutions do not traditionally serve.
  • OPFI +1.5% premarket to $11.37
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Brookdale appoints Nick Stengle as CEO
data.description:
• Proven Executive Brings Extensive Experience in Senior Living, Healthcare, and Hospitality and Track Record of Driving Durable, Sustainable Operational Excellence
  • Brookdale Senior Living (NYSE:BKD) has announced the appointment of Nick Stengle as Chief Executive Officer, effective October 6, 2025.
  • Mr. Stengle succeeds Denise W. Warren, who served as Interim CEO since April of 2025. Dennis will resume her role as Non-Executive Chairman of the Board.
  • BKD +1.1% after hours to $8.66
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Curbline Properties announces $250M share repurchase and $250M ATM program
data.description:
• Curbline Properties (NYSE:CURB) has authorized a $250 million share repurchase program.
  • In another event, Curbline Properties and its unit Curbline Properties LP entered into an ATM equity offering sales agreement having an aggregate offering price of up to $250M.
  • The net proceeds, from any sales of shares or upon physical settlement of any forward sale agreement, will be used for general corporate purposes, which may include, among other things, funding the acquisition of properties, working capital and capital expenditures, repaying outstanding indebtedness, or a combination of the foregoing.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Akamai expands partnership with Apiiro for comprehensive application security
U.S. to provide Ukraine intelligence on Russian energy infrastructure targets - report
data.description:
The U.S. will share intelligence with Ukraine for long-range missile strikes on energy infrastructure deep inside Russian territory, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing American officials.

U.S. officials are also asking NATO allies to provide similar support. The intelligence sharing will better equip Ukraine to target Russian refineries, pipelines, power stations and other infrastructure.

The U.S. is also weighing sending Ukraine powerful long-range missiles such as RTX (NYSE:RTX)-made Tomahawks and Anduril-manufactured Barracudas. No decision has been made yet.

The approval for additional intelligence came shortly before Trump said Ukraine is in a position to retake all of its seized territory, U.S. officials told WSJ.

Earlier this week, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said if the U.S. decides to supply Ukraine with Tomahawks, it will not change the military situation. 

Separately, European Union leaders are weighing using frozen Russian assets to provide a €140B loan to Ukraine. Moscow has called the plan "simply theft."

data.title: Job cuts fall 37% in September, YTD total higher than all of 2024
data.description:
U.S.-based employers announced 54,064 job cuts in September, a down 37% from August and 26% lower from the 72,821 announced in the same month last year, Challenger, Gray & Christmas said on Thursday.

So far in 2025, companies have announced 946,426 job cuts, the highest year-to-date total since 2020, when the pandemic led to 2.08M announced job cuts as entire industry sectors, such as leisure and hospitality, ground nearly to a halt. The 2025 year-to-date total is the fifth highest in the 36 years Challenger has reported.

The YTD total climbed 55% from the same period last year, and has already exceeded full-year 2024’s total by 24%.

“It’s very likely job cut plans are going to surpass a million for the first time since 2020 and for the ninth time in our series. Right now, we’re dealing with a stagnating labor market, cost increases, and a transformative new technology. With rate cuts on the way, we may see some stabilizing in the job market in the fourth quarter, but other factors could keep employers planning layoffs or holding off hiring,” said Andrew Challenger, senior vice president at Challenger, Gray & Christmas. 

Among industry sectors, services announced the most job cuts in August at 6,290, surging from 1,862 in August and 2,996 in September 2024. Year-to-date, the industry announced 61,590 job cuts, up 64% from the same period a year ago. 

The energy industry announced 5,807 in job cuts, bringing the year-to-date total to 14,811.

More on the US Labor Market

  • U.S. private sector employment pulls back in September: ADP jobs report

data.title: National Health Investors invests $74.3M to add 344 units in Oklahoma and Oregon to portfolio
S&P, Nasdaq futures tick up as job cuts data released early
data.description:
Stock index futures pointed to a slightly higher open Thursday as the U.S. government shutdown hit Day 2.

Info tech (XLK) was the biggest sector gainer before the bell, with Nvidia in the green. Some AI enthusiam continues as OpenAI became the world's most valuable startup.

The market will be without the usual Thursday jobless claims figures due to the shutdown. But Challenger reported (early due to a possible glitch) a 37% drop in September job cuts form August. But year to date cuts are the highest since 2020, with hiring the lowest since 2009. The report was scheduled to be released at 7:30 a.m. ET.

By the numbers

Dow (INDU) futures hovered near flat, down 11 points, or -0.02%, at 46,429.65. S&P 500 futures (SPX) gained 14.98 points, or 0.22%, to 6,726.18, while Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) outperformed, rising 92.67 points, or 0.37%, to 24,893.53.

The 2-year yield (US2Y) ticked up 1.4 basis points to 3.55% and the 5-year (US5Y) rose 0.3 bps to 3.68%, while the 10-year (US10Y) eased 0.2 bps to 4.10%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) also slipped, down 0.6 bps to 4.70%.

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) rose 0.45% to $3,882.38, silver (XAGUSD:CUR) added 0.33% to $47.48, while crude oil (CL1:COM) fell 0.55% to $61.44.
